title: i18n: externalize strings + translate core curriculum into 2 or more pilot languages (each language-reviewed)
context: A core audience for this curriculum is recent immigrants, refugees, and ESL learners who may be more comfortable in a language other than English. Translations make the curriculum accessible to these learners through partner organizations (e.g., refugee-resettlement programs). The pilot languages will be determined by the secured delivery partner's audience; this task first externalizes all strings to make translation possible, then produces translations into at least 2 pilot languages, each reviewed by both a language reviewer and a financial educator for accuracy. Translations are CC-BY-4.0 (or CC-BY-SA-4.0 where inherited) and credit translators.
objective: Externalize all curriculum strings into i18n resource files and produce reviewed translations of the core curriculum into at least 2 pilot languages, each language-reviewed for both linguistic accuracy and financial-content accuracy.
acceptanceCriteria: All learner-facing strings in the core curriculum are externalized into i18n resource files (e.g., JSON/YAML per language) — no hardcoded strings in components
Core curriculum (Budgeting, Saving, Debt, Banking, Scams modules + Glossary) translated into at least 2 pilot languages, with language selection driven by the secured delivery partner's audience
Each translation is reviewed by a language reviewer for linguistic accuracy and by a financial educator for financial-concept accuracy; reviews recorded per language per version
Translations inherit CC-BY-4.0 (or CC-BY-SA-4.0 where the source is share-alike); translators credited in the translators ledger with consent
Not-advice framing and non-commercial redirect are preserved and correctly translated in every language version
i18n build pipeline produces a language-selectable static site; language switching does not require a page reload or backend
output: A merged PR adding i18n string externalization and reviewed translations of the core curriculum in at least 2 pilot languages (content/i18n/), with a language-selectable static site build and translator credits.
